title: Governor Josh Stein Announces High-Speed Internet Access Coming in 2026 to 5,161 Rural North Carolinians
lede: Governor Josh Stein today announced nearly $26 million in awards through the state's Stop-Gap Solutions program that will connect 5,161 rural North Carolina homes, businesses, and community anchor institutions in 66 counties to high-speed internet infrastructure by the end of 2026.
mentionContext: "These broadband projects will ensure more families can soon access telehealth, students can complete their homework, businesses can compete in larger markets, and communities can thrive," said Governor Josh Stein. "I am committed to improving broadband access across the state and making sure no community is left behind."
fullText:
Governor Josh Stein today announced nearly $26 million in awards through the state's Stop-Gap Solutions program that will connect 5,161 rural North Carolina homes, businesses, and community anchor institutions in 66 counties to high-speed internet infrastructure by the end of 2026.

"These broadband projects will ensure more families can soon access telehealth, students can complete their homework, businesses can compete in larger markets, and communities can thrive," said Governor Josh Stein. "I am committed to improving broadband access across the state and making sure no community is left behind."

The Stop-Gap Solutions program, administered by the N.C. Department of Information Technology's (NCDIT) Division of Broadband and Digital Opportunity with federal American Rescue Plan Act funds, is designed to accelerate the expansion of high-speed internet infrastructure to eligible unserved and underserved households, businesses, community anchor institutions, and state facilities across North Carolina.

"High-speed internet access is the foundation for health care delivery, public safety operations, workforce development, and economic growth in our state," said Teena Piccione, NCDIT Secretary and State Chief Information Officer.

NCDIT has already contracted more than $670 million in broadband projects scheduled for completion this year to connect more than 252,000 homes and businesses, marking significant progress in expanding access statewide.

title: Governor Stein Awards Nearly $8.5 Million to Strengthen Small Business Infrastructure in Western North Carolina
lede: Governor Josh Stein today announced nearly $8.5 million in new infrastructure grants to support small business recovery in western North Carolina. The funding will assist 13 local projects through the North Carolina Department of Commerce's Small Business Infrastructure Grant Program (SmBIZ), continuing the state's coordinated response to the impacts of Hurricane Helene.
mentionContext: "Small businesses can't operate without reliable infrastructure," said Governor Josh Stein. "These investments will help communities repair essential systems, reduce future risk, and ensure local entrepreneurs have the dependable services they need to serve customers and grow. I thank the General Assembly for its partnership in funding these investments."
fullText:
Governor Josh Stein today announced nearly $8.5 million in new infrastructure grants to support small business recovery in western North Carolina. The funding will assist 13 local projects through the North Carolina Department of Commerce's Small Business Infrastructure Grant Program (SmBIZ), continuing the state's coordinated response to the impacts of Hurricane Helene.

"Small businesses can't operate without reliable infrastructure," said Governor Josh Stein. "These investments will help communities repair essential systems, reduce future risk, and ensure local entrepreneurs have the dependable services they need to serve customers and grow. I thank the General Assembly for its partnership in funding these investments."

The projects focus on restoring and improving public infrastructure systems that are vital to the success of small businesses – including water and sewer lines, stormwater controls, utility relocation, and downtown streetscape repairs.

With this announcement, the program has awarded nearly $30 million to support small business recovery from Hurricane Helene in western North Carolina since November 2025.

The SmBIZ program was created with the support of a $55 million appropriation from the North Carolina General Assembly. The program provides grants of up to $1 million to local governments to address infrastructure damage that directly affects small business districts and economic corridors.

title: In Charlotte, Governor Stein Calls for Law Enforcement Raises
lede: Governor Josh Stein joined law enforcement officials at Central Piedmont Community College to highlight urgent public safety funding needs in his proposed Critical Needs Budget.
mentionContext: "When our heroes are putting their lives on the line to serve and protect us, we must be just as committed to supporting them," the Governor stated. "My Critical Needs Budget invests in the people who keep North Carolinians safe by raising pay for troopers, correctional officers, and other state public safety professionals and ensuring agencies like the SBI and Department of Adult Correction have the resources they need to do their jobs effectively."
fullText:
Governor Josh Stein joined law enforcement officials at Central Piedmont Community College to highlight urgent public safety funding needs in his proposed Critical Needs Budget.

"When our heroes are putting their lives on the line to serve and protect us, we must be just as committed to supporting them," the Governor stated. "My Critical Needs Budget invests in the people who keep North Carolinians safe by raising pay for troopers, correctional officers, and other state public safety professionals and ensuring agencies like the SBI and Department of Adult Correction have the resources they need to do their jobs effectively."

The proposal allocates $211 million for public safety, including a 6.5% raise for juvenile court counselors and probation/parole officers, and 10% raises for correctional officers, law enforcement officers, and youth counselors. A 10% pay increase for nurses and behavioral health technicians is also included. North Carolina currently ranks 49th nationally in starting salaries for state troopers and correctional officers.

title: Governor Stein Celebrates PSA Airlines Headquarters Grand Opening in Charlotte
lede: Governor Josh Stein joined business leaders and elected officials at the grand opening for PSA Airlines' new headquarters in Charlotte. The new headquarters of the American Airlines subsidiary that operates American Eagle is expected to bring 400 jobs to Mecklenburg County and support more than $228 million in economic output in North Carolina each year.
mentionContext: "North Carolina is First in Flight and the future of flight," said Governor Josh Stein. "PSA Airlines' decision to move its headquarters to Charlotte is a testament to North Carolina's top-tier workforce and strong aviation industry. This investment will create good-paying jobs locally and keep our state connected to the world."
fullText:
Governor Josh Stein joined business leaders and elected officials at the grand opening for PSA Airlines' new headquarters in Charlotte. The new headquarters of the American Airlines subsidiary that operates American Eagle is expected to bring 400 jobs to Mecklenburg County and support more than $228 million in economic output in North Carolina each year.

"North Carolina is First in Flight and the future of flight," said Governor Josh Stein. "PSA Airlines' decision to move its headquarters to Charlotte is a testament to North Carolina's top-tier workforce and strong aviation industry. This investment will create good-paying jobs locally and keep our state connected to the world."

The American Airlines hub at Charlotte Douglas International Airport supports nearly 150,000 jobs and generates more than $30 billion in economic output across the state. In addition to supporting more than $228 million in economic output annually, PSA's new headquarters in Charlotte is expected to generate $10 million in state and local tax revenues each year.

More than 400 aerospace companies call North Carolina home. Last week, GE Aerospace announced it will invest $160 million across four North Carolina facilities, adding hundreds of new jobs.

title: Governor Stein Kicks Off Rural Listening Tour
lede: Governor Josh Stein kicked off his rural listening session series today. He met with business leaders, local elected officials, and other leaders to hear their concerns and discuss priorities for strengthening their communities.
mentionContext: "With more than 3.5 million people, North Carolina is home to the second-largest rural population in the country," said Governor Josh Stein. "We have more work to do to expand opportunity to every corner of the state because if you work hard, where you come from should never limit how far you can go. I'm grateful to hear directly from some of our rural residents about how we can work together on the issues affecting their daily lives."
fullText:
Governor Josh Stein kicked off his rural listening session series today. He met with business leaders, local elected officials, and other leaders to hear their concerns and discuss priorities for strengthening their communities. Governor Stein also stopped by Flavors on Main in Marion to meet with the owners and learn about their experience.

"With more than 3.5 million people, North Carolina is home to the second-largest rural population in the country," said Governor Josh Stein. "We have more work to do to expand opportunity to every corner of the state because if you work hard, where you come from should never limit how far you can go. I'm grateful to hear directly from some of our rural residents about how we can work together on the issues affecting their daily lives."

The tour represents the Stein Administration's whole-of-government approach to rural development. In the coming weeks and months, cabinet secretaries will host listening sessions across the state, focusing on health care, economic development and jobs, infrastructure, and more.

title: Governor Stein Encourages Visitors to Rediscover the Unforgettable Places of North Carolina at Visit NC Tourism Conference
lede: At Visit NC's annual tourism conference, North Carolina Governor Josh Stein celebrated the strength of North Carolina's tourism industry.
mentionContext: At Visit NC's annual tourism conference, North Carolina Governor Josh Stein celebrated the strength of North Carolina's tourism industry. He emphasized the state's diverse attractions across seasons — skiing in winter, swimming at the Outer Banks in summer, and NASCAR races in spring and fall.
fullText:
At Visit NC's annual tourism conference, North Carolina Governor Josh Stein celebrated the strength of North Carolina's tourism industry. He emphasized the state's diverse attractions across seasons — skiing in winter, swimming at the Outer Banks in summer, and NASCAR races in spring and fall.

The tourism industry significantly impacts North Carolina's economy. In 2024, domestic and international visitors spent $36.7 billion in the state. Daily visitor spending generates over $100 million for the state economy and $7.3 million in daily tax revenue. The tourism sector employs more than 230,000 North Carolinians and saves the average household nearly $600 annually in state and local taxes.

Recently, Governor Stein visited North Carolina's six ski resorts to highlight the mountains, which generate more than $244 million annually and attract over 780,000 visitors yearly. Last year, Governor Stein and First Lady Anna Stein launched the "Rediscover the Unforgettable" initiative to encourage travel to western North Carolina and support local economies.

title: Governor Stein Announces Boards and Commissions Appointments
lede: Governor Josh Stein announced appointments to multiple state boards and commissions.
mentionContext: Governor Josh Stein announced appointments to multiple state boards and commissions on March 12, 2026, including the Governor's Advisory Committee on Western North Carolina Recovery, the Juvenile Justice Planning Committee, the NCWorks Commission, and several professional licensing boards.
fullText: Governor Josh Stein announced appointments to multiple state boards and commissions on March 12, 2026. Appointments included the Governor's Advisory Committee on Western North Carolina Recovery, the Juvenile Justice Planning Committee, the NCWorks Commission, the NC Board of Examiners for Engineers and Surveyors, the NC Board of Physical Therapy Examiners, the NC Holocaust Council, the NC Interagency Coordinating Council for Children Birth to Five with Disabilities, the NC Partnership for Children, the NC Soil and Water Conservation Commission, the NC State Health Coordinating Council, the NC State Museum of Natural Sciences Advisory Commission, and the NC Symphony Society Board of Trustees.

title: Governor Stein Celebrates Exceptional North Carolinians at Long Leaf Pine Presentation
lede: Governor Josh Stein inducted 12 North Carolinians into The Order of the Long Leaf Pine for their contributions to the state. The award recognizes exemplary service to the state of North Carolina.
mentionContext: "These North Carolinians have selflessly spent their careers serving others and strengthening our communities," said Governor Josh Stein. "I am proud to honor them today for their dedication, service, and talent."
fullText:
Governor Josh Stein inducted 12 North Carolinians into The Order of the Long Leaf Pine for their contributions to the state. The award recognizes exemplary service to the state of North Carolina.

"These North Carolinians have selflessly spent their careers serving others and strengthening our communities," said Governor Josh Stein. "I am proud to honor them today for their dedication, service, and talent."

The 12 honorees include Karen Brinson Bell (Former Executive Director of the State Board of Elections), Kimberly Welborn (Director of the McDowell County Board of Elections), David Clinard (Founder of the D. Elwood Clinard Charitable Trust), Julia Clinard Townsend (Founder of the REACH Women's Network), Edward Finley (Former Chairman of the North Carolina Utilities Commission), Juvencio Rocha Peralta (Founder of the Association of Mexicans in North Carolina), Jenni Johnson (Magnet Coordinator at Hunter Elementary School), Polly Jones (Retired Ashe County High School English Teacher), Jim Clark (English Professor Emeritus at North Carolina State University), DeVetta Holman-Copeland (Coordinator of Student Resiliency and Academic Support at UNC Chapel Hill), Beverly McIver (Artist and Esbenshade Professor at Duke University), and Officer Percy Evans (Holly Springs Police Department).

Trees for the Triangle, a local tree planting nonprofit, is donating a longleaf pine tree seedling to each award recipient.
topics: Order of the Long Leaf Pine

title: Governor Stein Proposes $1.4 Billion Critical Needs Budget to Keep North Carolina Strong
lede: Governor Josh Stein called on the General Assembly to fast-track a $1.4 billion Critical Needs Budget focused on addressing North Carolina's most urgent needs while lawmakers continue work on a comprehensive state budget.
mentionContext: "North Carolina has gone nearly two and a half years without passing a new state budget – the only state in the country to finish 2025 without one," said Governor Josh Stein. "While lawmakers work toward a full, fiscally responsible budget this spring, there are urgent needs facing our state right now like fully funding Medicaid and giving law enforcement, teachers, and other public servants a long-deserved pay raise."
fullText:
Governor Josh Stein called on the General Assembly to fast-track a $1.4 billion Critical Needs Budget focused on addressing North Carolina's most urgent needs while lawmakers continue work on a comprehensive state budget.

"North Carolina has gone nearly two and a half years without passing a new state budget – the only state in the country to finish 2025 without one," said Governor Josh Stein. "While lawmakers work toward a full, fiscally responsible budget this spring, there are urgent needs facing our state right now like fully funding Medicaid and giving law enforcement, teachers, and other public servants a long-deserved pay raise. This budget invests in critical public safety, education and health care services for the people of North Carolina that cannot wait."

Medicaid provides health coverage for more than 3 million North Carolinians, including children, seniors, people with disabilities, expectant mothers, and working families. The Governor's budget recommends $319 million to fully fund Medicaid.

North Carolina ranks 49th in starting salaries for state troopers and correctional officers. The proposal includes pay raises for correctional officers, law enforcement officers, youth counselors, probation and parole officers, nurses, and behavioral health technicians.

The state remains in the bottom 10 nationally in average teacher pay. Governor Stein's budget raises starting and average teacher pay, restores master's degree pay, and increases compensation for senior teachers.

title: Governor Stein Proclaims March 2026 as Students@Work Month
lede: Governor Josh Stein proclaimed March 2026 to be Students@Work Month in North Carolina. Throughout the month, businesses across the state will educate about 25,000 middle school students about jobs and industries across North Carolina.
mentionContext: "If you work hard, where you come from in North Carolina should never limit how far you can go," said Governor Josh Stein. "It all starts with opening doors of opportunity for young people. For 16 years, Students@Work has helped to connect students to opportunities that allow them to explore their passions while preparing for the workforce, and I am pleased to celebrate their important work."
fullText:
Governor Josh Stein proclaimed March 2026 to be Students@Work Month in North Carolina. Throughout the month, businesses across the state will educate about 25,000 middle school students about jobs and industries across North Carolina.

"If you work hard, where you come from in North Carolina should never limit how far you can go," said Governor Josh Stein. "It all starts with opening doors of opportunity for young people. For 16 years, Students@Work has helped to connect students to opportunities that allow them to explore their passions while preparing for the workforce, and I am pleased to celebrate their important work."

During the month, businesses will provide virtual and in-person career awareness opportunities for North Carolina's middle school students. The North Carolina Business Committee for Education (NCBCE) and the North Carolina Department of Public Instruction are the primary partners for the Students@Work program, which is celebrating its 16th year.

Through Students@Work, students learn about careers in key industry sectors, including health care, hospitality, finance, skilled trades, manufacturing, entrepreneurship, technology, legal, biotechnology, education, construction, public service, and more.

Three state agencies are also participating in the program: the North Carolina Department of Environmental Quality, the North Carolina Department of Information Technology, and NCBCE, which operates out of the Office of the Governor.
